A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Using the paper_docs (query_db:2) and citations (query_db:5), the agent’s Python extraction (execute_python:20/22) incorrectly failed to detect publication years and relied on brittle keyword heuristics for contribution, resulting in zero qualifying papers and a premature return_answer claiming none exist despite the data supporting many matches.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Using query_db:14 and query_db:16 joined in execute_python:22, the agent applied overly restrictive regex heuristics to detect “empirical” contributions, which identified only two post‑2016 papers and thus returned an incomplete result list in return_answer:28 that does not match the ground-truth set.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent implemented brittle regex-based extraction (execute_python:26) that searched only the first 2000 characters for the publication year and used a simplistic keyword heuristic for "empirical," causing it to miss most qualifying papers and return only one result, which does not match the comprehensive ground-truth list.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python:46 the agent filtered papers by naively checking for the literal substring "empirical" in full text and taking the maximum 20xx year from the first 3000 characters as the publication year, then joined with aggregated totals from query_db:38—an incorrect implementation of metadata extraction that excluded most qualifying papers and led to wrong results.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

After extracting candidate empirical-after-2016 papers from MongoDB, the agent hard-coded a single title and only retrieved/returned its citations (execute_python:48 and return_answer:50), stopping early instead of listing all qualifying papers.
